entities.name: Francis Geisdorff
entities.context: Named in patent title notation as allottee, 1919 fee patent marked illegal

entities.name: Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
entities.context: Crow allottee #2336, deceased, allotted land in 1907, died November 27, 1918 of influenza, aged about 20, born May 6, 1898

entities.context: Father of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., received leasing papers from Superintendent Asbury in August 1918

entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., lived at Randlett/Fort Duchesne, Utah, heir to estate

entities.name: Emily M. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., also referenced as Emily M., care of Uintah and Ouray Agency

entities.name: Emily F. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., also referenced as Emily F. in May 1919 correspondence

entities.name: Emily Lucile Geisdorff
entities.context: Minor daughter of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and Emily J. Geisdorff, born May 23, 1918

entities.name: Thomas R. Powers
entities.context: Purchaser of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment, of Wyola, Montana and Hartman, Wyoming, bid $1500 for 159.67 acres

entities.context: Superintendent of Crow Agency, Montana, handled land sale proceedings

entities.context: President of the United States who signed fee patent No. 719146 on November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers

entities.name: John McPhaul
entities.context: Acting Assistant Commissioner of General Land Office, signed clearance for fee patent issuance, October 13, 1919

entities.name: Eloise Whitebear Pease
entities.context: Collection owner whose name appears on collection stamps throughout documents

entities.name: Crow Agency
entities.context: U.S. Indian Service agency in Montana handling allotment and land sale matters

entities.name: Uintah and Ouray Agency
entities.context: U.S. Indian Service agency at Fort Duchesne, Utah, under whose jurisdiction Frederick Geisdorff Jr. lived

entities.name: Office of Indian Affairs
entities.context: Federal bureau overseeing Indian land sales and allotments, Washington D.C.

entities.name: General Land Office
entities.context: Federal office responsible for issuing patents, located in Washington D.C. and Billings, Montana

entities.name: Department of the Interior
entities.context: Federal department overseeing Indian affairs and land patents

entities.name: Crow Tribe of Indians
entities.context: Native American tribe whose reservation land was subject to allotment; argued to be rightful owner upon allottee's death

entities.context: Bank holding $7.98 lease money deposited to credit of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s estate

entities.context: Bank on which Thomas R. Powers' checks dated November 2, 1918 were drawn

entities.context: Montana reservation where Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment was located

entities.name: Fort Duchesne, Utah
entities.context: Location where Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and his widow Emily lived, under Uintah and Ouray jurisdiction

entities.name: Randlett, Utah
entities.context: Location where Frederick Geisdorff Jr. was living at time of allotment sale proceedings

entities.context: Location of Office of Indian Affairs and General Land Office headquarters

entities.context: Location of U.S. Land Office and Merchants National Bank

events.description: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotted 159.67 acres (S½ NE¼, SE¼ NW¼, Lot 2, Sec. 19, T.9S., R.34E.) under Act of April 11, 1882; trust patent issued December 2, 1907 under Act of February 8, 1887
Francis Geisdorff allotted 648.04 acres as homestead under Act of June 4, 1920; trust patent No. 227584 signed by President Calvin Coolidge on December 21, 1923
Petition for Sale of Land by original allottee Frederick Geisdorff Jr. received at Crow Agency
Thomas R. Powers made an offer to lease Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment and adjacent land owned by relatives
Government Farmer appraised Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment at $1,194 in connection with Powers' lease/purchase request
Superintendent C.H. Asbury wrote to Frederick Geisdorff Sr. sending leasing papers and requesting response
Crow Agency offered Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment for sale on petition acknowledged before Supt. Kneale of Uintah and Ouray Agency
Acceptance of sale of allotment sent to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. for signature
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. signed and returned acceptance form to Crow office
Thomas R. Powers notified that owners of land under Crow Land Sale No. 991 accepted his bid of $1,500 for 159.67 acres; balance of purchase price demanded
Superintendent acknowledged receipt of two checks from Thomas R. Powers for $1,350 and $150 (balance of purchase price and advertising fee)
Superintendent wrote to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. noting he was only 20 years of age and doubting Indian Bureau would approve sale; requested birth date and relatives' names
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. died of influenza, aged about 20, intestate, survived by wife Emily J. Geisdorff and minor daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Superintendent Kneale wrote to Crow Agency notifying that Fritz (Frederick Geisdorff Jr.) died a few days ago of influenza
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Thomas R. Powers explaining age issue with allottee and offering to cancel sale and return payment
Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff wrote to Crow Agency Superintendent inquiring if agency could help pay a note owed by her deceased husband
Superintendent responded to Emily J. Geisdorff that no funds were available and that a hearing would be advertised to determine heirs
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner of Indian Affairs reviewing all matters of land sale, noting allottee born May 6, 1898, and holding two checks from Powers totaling $1,500
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Emily M. Geisdorff enclosing copy of letter to Commissioner and asking if she wished sale completed
Uintah and Ouray Agency notified Superintendent Asbury of birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff, born May 23, 1918
Office of Indian Affairs responded to Asbury's March 24 letter stating nothing shows brother/sister want to sell allotments or widow wants sale to proceed; authorized proceeding if heirs want sale
Superintendent Asbury enclosed sale papers to Commissioner stating he received acceptance of widow of allottee
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Emily F. Geisdorff asking whether she wished sale completed to Powers for $1,500 or bid rejected and land readvertised
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner requesting approval of completion of Land Sale No. 991, noting Powers requested return of deposit if no action taken
General Land Office (John McPhaul, Acting Assistant Commissioner) stated no reservations or withdrawals covering land and no reasons why fee patent should not be issued to purchaser
Fee Patent No. 719146 issued by President Woodrow Wilson to Thomas R. Powers for 159.67 acres of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment No. 2336
Office of Indian Affairs transmitted fee patent No. 719146 to Superintendent Asbury for delivery to Thomas R. Powers upon signed receipt
Superintendent notified U.S. Land Office Billings of fee patent No. 719146 issued November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers on allotment No. 2336
Superintendent wrote to Emily J. Geisdorff explaining proceeds of sale could be transferred to her agency after heirs determined; minor's shares require special authority
Hearing held to determine heirs of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; testimony taken by Superintendent Kneale at Fort Duchesne
Report on heirship transmitted to Secretary of Interior regarding estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., Crow allottee No. 2336
Heirs of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. determined; Emily J. Geisdorff entitled to one-half plus dower interest; Emily Lucile Geisdorff entitled to balance
Check No. 10613 for $1,317.26 sent to Emily J. Geisdorff representing her share of estate proceeds; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily
Report prepared analyzing the Geisdorff land sale as illegal, arguing land should have reverted to Crow Tribe upon allottee's death per trust patent terms and Treaty of 1968

financial_transactions.payee: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. estate / Crow Agency
financial_transactions.description: Purchase price for 159.67 acres of Crow allotment No. 2336 under Crow Land Sale No. 991; paid as $150 deposit with bid and $1,350 balance check dated November 2, 1918 on Sheridan Banking Company
Advertising fee for land sale (purchase price over $1,000 and under $2,000); paid by check on Sheridan Banking Company
Heirship fee deducted from estate proceeds as authorized by Indian Office
Check No. 10613 representing widow's share (one-half plus dower interest) of estate proceeds from land sale; total on books was $1,521.24 less $15 heirship fee
Daughter's share of estate proceeds from land sale, held to credit of minor daughter
Lease money deposited to credit of estate in Merchants National Bank of Billings, Montana
Total amount on books at Crow Agency to credit of Frederick Geisdorff estate before deduction of heirship fee
Appraised value of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment as determined by Government Farmer
Ten percent deposit submitted with bid for Crow Land Sale No. 991
Balance of purchase price paid by check on Sheridan Banking Company, acknowledged received November 5, 1918

correspondence.subject: Leasing papers for allotment
correspondence.action_requested: Response to leasing papers for Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment and adjacent lands
correspondence.outcome: No action reflected in files at that time

correspondence.subject: Acceptance of bid for Crow Land Sale No. 991
correspondence.action_requested: Remit balance of purchase price within 30 days plus advertising fee; sign and return affidavit of vendee in duplicate
correspondence.outcome: Powers paid balance; checks received November 5, 1918

correspondence.subject: Age issue with land sale approval
correspondence.action_requested: Submit date of birth and names/addresses of all relatives; obtain statement from Superintendent Kneale recommending sale approval
correspondence.outcome: No reply received; allottee died November 27, 1918

correspondence.subject: Age issue with Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment sale
correspondence.action_requested: Informed Powers of age issue; offered to cancel sale and return payment upon request
correspondence.outcome: Sale not immediately cancelled; proceedings continued

correspondence.subject: Death of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.outcome: Crow Agency informed of allottee's death

correspondence.subject: Request for assistance paying note owed by deceased husband
correspondence.action_requested: Financial assistance to pay husband's debt

correspondence.subject: Response to request for financial assistance
correspondence.outcome: Informed no funds available; hearing to be advertised to determine heirs

correspondence.subject: Review of land sale matters for Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment
correspondence.action_requested: Instructions on how to proceed with sale given allottee's death and minority at time of sale

correspondence.subject: Proposed sale of allotment and combined sale possibility
correspondence.action_requested: Inform whether widow wishes sale completed

correspondence.subject: Response to March 24 letter regarding Frederick Geisdorff Jr. land sale
correspondence.action_requested: Prepare and forward sale papers if probable heirs want sale to proceed; otherwise reject bid and readvertise after heirs determined
correspondence.outcome: Asbury proceeded with sale papers after obtaining widow's acceptance

correspondence.subject: Inquiry whether widow wishes sale to Powers completed or bid rejected
correspondence.action_requested: Sign enclosed authority if she wishes sale to proceed; write if she does not

correspondence.subject: Enclosure of sale papers for allotment 2336 with widow's acceptance
correspondence.outcome: Sale approved; fee patent issued November 14, 1919

correspondence.subject: Proceeds of land sale and minor's share
correspondence.action_requested: Informed widow proceeds can be transferred to her agency upon request after heirs determined; minor's share requires special authority

correspondence.subject: Copy of letter to Indian Bureau regarding Geisdorff sale
Completion of Land Sale No. 991
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale at early date; noted Powers requesting return of deposit if no action taken
correspondence.outcome: Sale approved; fee patent issued November 14, 1919

correspondence.subject: Transmittal of fee patent No. 719146 to Thomas R. Powers
correspondence.action_requested: Make proper notations on records; deliver patent to Powers upon signed receipt; forward original receipt to Washington

correspondence.subject: Notification of fee patent No. 719146 issued to Thomas R. Powers
Heirship report for estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., Crow allottee No. 2336
correspondence.action_requested: Consideration and approval of heirship determination

correspondence.subject: Distribution of estate proceeds
correspondence.outcome: Check No. 10613 for $1,317.26 sent to widow; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily

legislative_actions.outcome: Dawes Act; basis for trust patent issued December 2, 1907 for Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment; trust patent language specified land reverts to United States if allottee dies before expiration of trust period
Act under which Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment was made as amended
Referenced in fee patent No. 719146 issued to Thomas R. Powers; related to water rights and reclamation
Act under which Francis Geisdorff's homestead allotment of 648.04 acres was made; trust period of 25 years; coal, oil, gas and mineral deposits reserved for benefit of Crow Indian Tribe
Cited in June 1979 report as basis for Crow Tribe's ownership claim; argued allotted land was first and foremost property of Crow Tribe and should have reverted upon allottee's death
